sn15.11:1.3: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.11:1.4: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.11:1.5: When you see someone who has fallen on hard times, overwhelmed with hard times, you should conclude:
sn15.11:1.6: ‘We, too, have experienced just this sort of thing in the course of that long, long time.’
sn15.11:1.7: “Why is that? From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on. A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on. Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—
sn15.11:1.8: enough to become disenchanted with all fabricated things, enough to become dispassionate, enough to be released.”
